summary: 8th-century Northumbrian monk at Wearmouth-Jarrow. 'Father of English history' for his Ecclesiastical History of the English People. Popularized BC/AD dating. Wrote On the Reckoning of Time and a five-line Death Song.

works.name: Ecclesiastical History of the English People
works.description: Landmark history of Christianity in Britain, written in the 730s.

clues.clue: Ecclesiastical History — chronicles Christianity's growth in Britain
examples: Ecclesiastical History of the English People

clues.clue: Synod of Whitby — Easter date controversy
Preserved the story of Caedmon and his Hymn
examples: related how the cowherd Caedmon miraculously received the ability to sing

clues.clue: Gregory the Great's pun: 'not Angles, but Angels'
examples: Gregory thought they were 'not Angles, but angels'
known as the father of English history

clues.clue: Monk at Wearmouth-Jarrow who almost never left
Only native of Great Britain made a Doctor of the Church
examples: the only native of Great Britain to be made a Doctor of the Church

works.name: On the Reckoning of Time and Other
works.description: Computus work; popularized BC/AD dating. Also wrote Death Song, De natura rerum, riddles.

clues.clue: Popularized BC/AD dating system (invented by Dionysius Exiguus)
examples: popularized a system invented by Dionysius Exiguus

clues.clue: On the Reckoning of Time — computus for Easter dates
examples: engaged in the practice of computus

clues.clue: Death Song — five-line poem pondering 'good and evil within his soul'
examples: states 'before the unavoidable journey,' no one is wiser

clues.clue: Died Ascension Day 735; asked for 'pepper, napkins, and incense'
examples: asked for 'some pepper, and napkins, and some incense'

clues.clue: First to correlate tides to the moon
examples: first person to correlate the movement of tides to stages of the moon

clues.clue: Falsely accused of heresy over Easter calculations
examples: Bishop Wilfrid's minions falsely accused him of heresy

clues.clue: Teacher of Archbishop Egbert; mentor of Alcuin of York
Explained Easter customs with Germanic goddess Eostre
examples: explained Easter customs with the Germanic goddess Eostre

comprehensive_summary: The Venerable Bede is an 8th-century Northumbrian monk at Wearmouth-Jarrow, called 'the father of English history.' His Ecclesiastical History of the English People chronicles Christianity's spread in Britain and is the source for Gregory the Great's 'not Angles, but Angels' pun, the Synod of Whitby's Easter controversy, and Caedmon's story. His On the Reckoning of Time deals with computus, and his writings popularized BC/AD dating. He was first to correlate tides to the moon. He died on Ascension Day 735 after finishing his translation of the Gospel of John, asking for 'pepper, napkins, and incense.' He is the only British native made a Doctor of the Church.
